meta.description: Identify independent prognostic factors by univariate and stepwise multivariate Cox proportional hazards regression. Inputs: survival time, event status, clinical and molecular predictor variables. Outputs: HR table, forest plot, p-values, proportional-hazards test.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated scientific claims, invented numeric outputs, or unsupported conclusions were observed across the audit inputs.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed inside research-analysis scope and did not provide diagnosis or treatment advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflow uses appropriate Cox regression and explicit insufficiency checks for event-poor input.

code_usability.detail: All valid runs were executable and invalid inputs failed cleanly with actionable SKILL_* errors.

functional_suitability.note: The skill covers the full promised Cox-analysis and forest-plot workflow with aligned CLI, outputs, examples, and retained expected outputs.

reliability.note: Input validation, overwrite protection, deterministic execution, retained expected outputs, and controlled analytical failures are all present.

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure is used correctly and the references/scripts split keeps heavy detail out of the main trigger surface.

agent_usability.note: The added When to Use and When Not to Use sections make the skill easier to trigger and apply correctly from a cold start.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural, though the task remains specialized to survival-analysis users.

security.note: Inputs are validated and no unsafe execution patterns were observed; full local paths are intentionally logged for provenance.

maintainability.note: Responsibilities are now cleanly split across CLI, validation, IO, core Cox helpers, plotting, orchestration, and utilities, and every script remains within the 150-line project guideline.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision, progressive disclosure, idempotent reruns, escape boundaries, and preserved expected outputs are all strong.

inputs.label: Default cohort analysis
inputs.note: Completed successfully and produced the documented tables, serialized data artifact, and session metadata.

assertions.text: The documented analyze CLI path is runnable as described.
assertions.note: Rscript scripts/main.R analyze completed successfully.

assertions.text: The run emits the documented table and data artifacts.
assertions.note: Both Cox tables and analysis_data.rds were present.

assertions.text: The workflow records reproducibility metadata.
assertions.note: session_info.txt captured parameters, package versions, and the seed.

assertions.text: The output stays within the stated survival-analysis scope.
assertions.note: The run produced Cox analysis artifacts only, without nomogram or ROC behavior.

inputs.label: Skip-univariate fit
inputs.note: Accepted a reduced feature set and skip_univariate mode while preserving a valid multivariable output.

assertions.text: The skill accepts valid feature subsets.
assertions.note: The reduced feature list parsed successfully.

assertions.text: skip_univariate changes the workflow without breaking output generation.
assertions.note: The run omitted the univariate table and still wrote the multivariable table.

assertions.text: The multivariable output remains interpretable.
assertions.note: The feature column resolved to source features such as age, stage, and risk.

assertions.text: The run remains deterministic and auditable.
assertions.note: The seeded invocation completed and wrote session_info.txt.

inputs.label: No-event cohort rejection
inputs.note: Correctly rejected the event-free cohort before model fitting with a specific validation error for insufficient events.

assertions.text: The skill rejects event-free cohorts before fitting Cox models.
assertions.note: Execution stopped during input validation.

assertions.text: The failure message is specific and actionable.
assertions.note: The error explicitly identifies the missing minimum event count.

assertions.text: The failure does not fabricate analytical output.
assertions.note: No result tables or PDFs were created for the invalid run.

assertions.text: The failure remains within the documented boundary behavior.
assertions.note: The rejection matches the documented invalid-parameter contract for insufficient-event input.

inputs.label: Univariate forest plot
inputs.note: Rendered a non-empty single-page univariate forest plot and wrote plotting session metadata.

assertions.text: The plotting command accepts the exported univariate Excel table.
assertions.note: The command completed without conversion steps.

assertions.text: The plot output is materially present rather than blank.
assertions.note: uni_forest_plot.pdf was created with non-trivial file size.

assertions.text: Plotting preserves reproducibility metadata.
assertions.note: A plotting-specific session_info.txt was written.

assertions.text: The plotting step remains decoupled from model fitting.
assertions.note: The command read an existing result table rather than refitting Cox models.

inputs.label: Dual custom plot generation
inputs.note: Accepted custom sizing parameters and produced both univariate and multivariable PDFs successfully.

assertions.text: The plot commands accept custom sizing parameters.
assertions.note: Width, height, and font size were parsed and recorded in session_info.txt.

assertions.text: The multivariable plotting path is runnable on the exported multivariable table.
assertions.note: multi_forest_plot.pdf was produced successfully.

assertions.text: Dual plot generation remains stable in repeated execution.
assertions.note: Both plot commands completed sequentially without overwrite or device errors.

assertions.text: The outputs remain inside the documented plotting scope.
assertions.note: The run only generated the requested forest plots and metadata file.

key_strengths: The skill now matches the expected data-analysis layout closely, including dedicated CLI, validation, IO, plotting, orchestration, references, and retained expected outputs.
The analytical workflow is reproducible: seeded execution, parameter capture, and per-run session_info files are all present for both analysis and plotting.
The R CLI contract is stronger after the standards refactor: command entry, package checks, and documented test paths are aligned for mode-B execution.
The retained tests/expected_output bundle makes the skill easier to review and validate alongside the runnable smoke test.

recommendations.title: Add testthat smoke assertions
recommendations.problem: The retained tests/run_smoke_test.R script is a solid executable smoke test, but it is still a custom assertion harness rather than an integrated testthat suite.
recommendations.root_cause: The skill prioritizes a simple runnable smoke workflow over a formal unit-test framework.
recommendations.fix: Add one small testthat file that calls the smoke workflow in a temporary directory and asserts the expected artifact manifest.

recommendations.title: Trim top-level SKILL length slightly
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md is now complete and clear, but it remains longer than many focused data-analysis skills.
recommendations.root_cause: Detailed examples, testing instructions, and output documentation are all kept in the top-level document.
recommendations.fix: Move one or two longer example blocks into references/cli-guide.md while keeping the primary trigger and core usage path in SKILL.md.
